steps.title: Identify the direction of the comparison
steps.content:
Comparing the placebo-aware group to the control group for each measure:
Global improvement: 5.0 vs 3.9
Symptom severity reduction: 92.00 vs 46.00
Quality of life improvement: 11.4 vs 5.4

All three measures are higher for the placebo-aware group — a consistent upward pattern across all measures.

steps.title: Match the pattern to the answer
steps.content:
Choice D states the ratings “were higher for all three measures,” which is exactly what the comparison shows.

Since the passage defines higher ratings as greater well-being, this choice correctly indicates greater overall well-being.

The other choices either reverse the direction (B), cite wrong numbers (C), or avoid comparison (A).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
